intro: The clearest split between Brevo and Amazon SES is vendor lock-in: Brevo is rated low (Marketing/email, SMTP portable), while Amazon SES is rated high (SMTP-compatible, portable to other email providers). Brevo is Marketing and transactional email/SMS platform with automation, CRM, and a free tier API; it fits best for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Amazon SES is AWS scalable transactional and bulk email sending service with SMTP and API access; it fits best for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Pick Brevo for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts); pick Amazon SES for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Brevo is not for internal projects. Amazon SES is not for internal projects. Honest trade-offs: Brevo is weak for internal team communication, and Amazon SES is weak for internal team communication — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: Brevo is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); Amazon SES is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Brevo if low lock-in (Marketing/email, SMTP portable) is acceptable and you need transactional emails (receipts, alerts).
verdict_b: Choose Amazon SES if high lock-in (SMTP-compatible, portable to other email providers) is acceptable and you need transactional emails (receipts, alerts).

faq.q: Is Brevo cheaper than Amazon SES?
faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Brevo offers a free tier. Amazon SES has a free tier with paid plans starting at $50 per month.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Brevo to Amazon SES?
faq.a: Migration may be non-trivial: Amazon SES is rated high lock-in (SMTP-compatible, portable to other email providers). Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Both score 4/5 on developer experience in our data. Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Amazon SES a good alternative to Brevo?
faq.a: Amazon SES sits in the same category as Brevo, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Amazon SES is best fit for transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Note: Amazon SES is not for internal projects.
